summary: Build M4 campaign planning, supply forecast, and war report UI
acceptance_criteria: Client read models expose campaign objective, muster readiness, mobilized commitments, grain supply, route/transport forecast, march status, reinforcement timing, siege choice, withdrawal, AI reason, and war report summaries without storing full WorldState in Redux/client store.

UI supports planning a campaign before the rainy season, reviewing predicted supply and route risks, submitting plan/cancel/withdraw/siege commands through Worker/protocol paths, and seeing rejection or risk reasons.
War report UI shows automatic engagement/siege outcome, losses, supply state, withdrawal reason, and postwar governance handoff candidate without duplicating sim-core formulas.
Map/route visualization may use existing map-renderer surfaces, but React must not own per-entity Pixi DisplayObjects and Pixi state must remain non-authoritative.
Storybook or Playwright coverage exercises planning, command submission, rejection/reason display, siege/withdrawal choice, AI reason display, and war report rendering.
No Electron/Node integration, full diplomacy UI, manual battle UI, 3D battle, telemetry, or new production dependency is introduced.
required_tests: Storybook story or Playwright test covers M4 campaign planning, supply/route forecast, siege/withdrawal command flow, AI reasons, and war report rendering.
node -e JSON parse/shape check for M4-CLIENT-CAMPAIGN-PLANNING-UI-001
notes: If UI needs missing command/query fields, route back to the relevant simulation task; do not reimplement domain rules in React.
Keep the first screen task-focused and operational; this is not a landing page or marketing page.

Explicit exclusions for M4 remain: manual node battle, complex naval warfare, full diplomacy, 3D battle, multiplayer/server, telemetry, arbitrary-code mods, and frozen product or architecture changes.
